personas.name: Solo founder making hire #1
personas.icp: Bootstrapped or post-incorporation SG founder (typically 30s-40s, 6-18 months post-incorporation) about to hire their first SG citizen / PR or WP employee — usually a junior ops / sales / engineering role in a tech, agency, design, or e-commerce micro-firm. A stranger reading this names: an Antler / Iterative / pre-seed Wavemaker founder who just closed angel cheque, a SG e-commerce store owner expanding past Shopify-solo mode, or a local agency lead transitioning from freelancer to employer.
pains: Has read the IRAS + CPF + MOM websites for 4 hours and still doesn't understand the difference between IR8A AIS-mandatory and voluntary, or whether their first hire triggers WICA cover
The accountant they use for tax filings charges S$80-150/mo for monthly payroll on top — and the founder isn't sure that's even the right fee or what it covers
Phone-tree and email-back-in-3-days support from CPF Board / IRAS / MOM hotlines means a missed monthly deadline = a S$300-1,000 fine they didn't budget for
Has seen Deel / Remote pricing pages, calculated USD 200-400/mo per employee, and immediately closed the tab — feels like enterprise tooling at small-business cost
Doesn't want to set up an HR function — they started the business to do the thing they're good at, not to be an employer-of-record
personas.current_workaround: Either does CPF EZPay submission themselves on the 14th of each month (45 min including ringing CPF hotline once for the new-employer registration step), pays an external accountant S$50-150/mo as a per-payroll add-on to their tax retainer, or 'borrows' the bookkeeping from a friend who runs a similar micro-firm and copy-pastes the same Excel template.

nba.summary: DIY accountant retainer + founder time on monthly statutory submissions + occasional fine for missed deadline / wrong calc. The displaced spend isn't large but it's annoying and unpredictable.

tooling_lines.tool: Founder time on CPF EZPay + IRAS submissions (1 hr/mo @ S$50/hr loaded)
Annualised buffer for late-fee / fine exposure (typical S$200/yr / 12)

personas.name: Micro services / agency owner (2-5 staff)
personas.icp: Owner-operator of a SG micro-firm in professional services / design / consulting / digital agency / IT services with 2-5 SG-resident staff (mostly PMET, all on Pte Ltd payroll). Typically 35-50 years old, has been running the firm 3-7 years, hires #2 was an inflection point that exposed the DIY-payroll cracks. A stranger reading this names: a typical ASME-SBF member law firm or accounting practice, a small architecture studio, a 4-person creative agency in Tanjong Pagar, or an IT contractor's roll-up.
pains: Currently uses Talenox or Payboy at SGD 4-7/employee/mo and finds the actual configuration burden (CPF rates by age band, OW vs AW, AIS submission setup) more than they want to keep doing
Cannot decide whether to hire a part-time HR contractor (~S$2-3k/mo) or upgrade their accountant to a full service retainer (~S$300-500/mo) or just keep DIYing it
Annual IR8A AIS submission week is uniformly hated — 4-6 evenings of cross-referencing payslips against the AIS feed
Firm is approaching the S$1M GST registration threshold and the founder knows that triggers more scrutiny on payroll cleanliness during any forward IRAS audit
Wants the WICA, CPF, IR8A, and ad-hoc HR letters (employment confirmation, salary verification for visa / loan) to be a single phone call, not three vendors
personas.current_workaround: Talenox or Payboy SaaS subscription (~SGD 12-35/mo for 3-5 employees) + accountant retainer that handles bookkeeping + monthly CPF + annual IR8A AIS (S$200-400/mo blended for SME bookkeeping + payroll) + separate WICA broker (annualised S$240-600/yr) + occasional law-firm letter at S$150-400 per employment-confirmation document.

nba.summary: SaaS subscription + accountant retainer + WICA broker + ad-hoc legal letters. The owner is paying ~S$300-450/mo today across 3-4 line items and doesn't want any of them but doesn't have time to consolidate.

tooling_lines.tool: Talenox / Payboy / HReasily payroll SaaS (S$25/mo for 4 employees)
Accountant retainer covering payroll + CPF + IR8A (S$280/mo midpoint)
Ad-hoc HR letters / employment confirmations (avg S$300/yr / 12)

personas.name: F&B / retail owner with WP/S-Pass mix
personas.icp: Owner of a SG F&B outlet (restaurant, cafe, cloud kitchen, bubble-tea / dessert chain) or boutique retail / beauty salon with 4-10 staff including 1-3 Work Permit / S-Pass foreign workers + part-time / casual SG staff. Typically 30s-50s, owner-operator on the floor most days, sees payroll + WP renewals as the single most stressful monthly job. A stranger reading this names: a typical SRA / RAS member single-outlet operator, a bubble-tea chain founder pre-Series-A, a Singaporean-Chinese family bakery, or a beauty / nail salon group with 2-3 outlets.
pains: WP / S-Pass levy is auto-debited from their bank account but renewal calendar lives in MOM Self-Help Portal that they only log into when there's a problem — missed renewal once, fined S$5,000 + worker had to leave the country
Casual / part-time payslip generation requires hourly rate × hours worked × CPF age-band logic that Talenox / Payboy charge per-employee for, even when the staff member only worked 3 days that month
Foreign worker on S-Pass earning S$3,200/mo means CPF only on SG citizens / PR — the rule is simple but the payroll software keeps prompting confusing options
Owner is on the floor 12-14 hours / day; doing payroll on their phone after closing time is the actual workflow, not a desktop SaaS dashboard
Want a single 'WP renewal coming in 30 days' calendar / SMS push integrated with the actual payroll, not three separate apps
personas.current_workaround: Mix of Talenox / Payboy / HReasily payroll SaaS (~SGD 35-60/mo for 6-8 employees) + manual MOM Self-Help Portal logins for WP / S-Pass renewals + outsourced bookkeeper at S$300-700/mo for monthly accounts + WICA broker (S$50-90/mo annualised) + S-Pass agent at S$200-500 per pass for new applications and renewals (engaged when the owner gives up trying to do it themselves).

nba.summary: Payroll SaaS + bookkeeper + WICA broker + S-Pass agent + owner's own time. The owner is paying ~S$500-700/mo across 4-5 line items plus their own out-of-hours time — the real NBA is 'I want to close this app and go to bed'.

tooling_lines.tool: Payroll SaaS for 6-8 staff incl. casuals (S$50/mo)
External bookkeeper retainer covering monthly accounts + payroll review (S$450/mo midpoint)
WICA broker annual fee for outlet + staff (S$840/yr / 12)
S-Pass / WP agent annualised (avg S$1,200/yr blended across 2 passes / 12)
Owner time on monthly payroll + MOM portal (3 hr/mo @ S$60/hr loaded)

personas.name: Small accounting firm (white-label channel)
personas.icp: Sole-prac or 2-8 partner SG accounting / corp-sec firm whose book includes 20-150 micro-firm clients each running 1-10 employees. Typically ICPAS / ISCA member firm, sells monthly bookkeeping + tax + corp-sec but treats payroll as the unprofitable tail their clients still demand. Owner-partner is 40s-60s; payroll is delegated to a junior staffer who is overworked and error-prone. A stranger reading this names: a typical ICPAS member sole-prac in Bukit Timah, a 4-partner Tier-3 firm in Tanjong Pagar that grew out of a Big-4 alumni split, or a Sleek / Osome competitor at the smaller end of the corp-sec channel.
pains: Monthly payroll for client book is the last hour of every weekday — junior staffer is the bottleneck; partners can't bill the time at audit rates
Software seat licenses (Talenox / Payboy / HReasily) cost the firm SGD 4-7 × 5 staff × 100 client-firms = a meaningful overhead they pass to clients at unhappy mark-ups
Clients call the firm directly when CPF / WICA / IR8A goes wrong; the firm is liable even when the underlying SaaS errored — but the firm isn't the system-of-record
Wants to white-label a clean, accountant-friendly EOR layer that handles the client-facing compliance noise so the firm can focus on tax + audit + advisory billing

Will switch the entire client book to one provider only if the per-firm wholesale price is meaningfully below the current Talenox / Payboy line — and only if there's a partner portal with bulk-management
personas.current_workaround: Multiple Talenox / Payboy / HReasily seat subscriptions across the client book + junior staffer salary cost (S$2,500-4,000/mo loaded for the dedicated payroll-clerk function) + partner-time exception handling + whatever mark-up the firm dares pass through to clients. Net economics: payroll is a break-even or loss-leading service the firm offers to retain the bookkeeping / tax / corp-sec work.

nba.summary: Per-firm wholesale comparison: existing payroll SaaS seats average SGD 25-40/firm/mo all-in once the firm's mark-up is washed out; the all-in cost-of-delivery to the firm is SGD 60-100/firm once junior-staffer time + exception handling is loaded.

tooling_lines.tool: Existing payroll SaaS seat per client firm (Talenox / Payboy avg)
Junior staffer time per client firm (S$3k/mo / 100 firms)

pricing_models.name: Per-firm flat monthly fee (with employee-tier ladder)
pricing_models.rationale: Fits the SG micro-firm cost-paranoia threshold (S$59/S$129/S$249 all sit below S$300/mo casual-sign-up ceiling per Indie Hackers SG / SBF Founder Pulse). Per-firm pricing decouples the founder's cost from headcount growth — a feature, not a bug, because micro-firms don't scale linearly. Aligned with global EOR comp set's headline tier behaviour (single seat reference price) but inverted: we charge per firm, they charge per employee.

pricing_models.name: Per-employee seat-based (Talenox / Deel pattern)
pricing_models.rationale: Standard global EOR + most SG payroll SaaS pattern. Conditions the founder to scale-with-growth — but at S$59/employee/mo (Talenox-equivalent floor for managed compliance) a 6-staff F&B firm pays S$354/mo, which exits the casual-sign-up threshold immediately. Penalises high-headcount-low-margin segments (F&B, retail, construction) which are exactly where Pro-tier WP/S-Pass concierge fits.

pricing_models.name: Hybrid: low base fee + per-employee overage above threshold
pricing_models.rationale: Honest middle: S$59/mo base for first 3 employees, S$15/employee/mo above. Maps to micro-firm reality (most under 3 staff). Adds a calculator on the pricing page (modest UX cost). Useful for the F&B / retail tier where staff count varies seasonally — but introduces a 'gotcha' line item the cost-paranoid founder doesn't love.

pricing_models.name: Annual prepay with discount (-15-20% vs monthly)
pricing_models.rationale: Variant overlay on the per-firm tier — captures churn-resistance + working-capital benefit (Elitez Group pre-funds 12 months of compliance ops) + creates a price-anchor ('S$590/yr' reads cheaper than 'S$59/mo × 12'). Industry standard SaaS lever. Should be available on every tier; not a tier in itself.

pricing_models.name: Outcome-based (% of payroll value)
pricing_models.rationale: Maximum alignment, minimum adoption — SG micro-firm founders read '% of payroll' as 'they get a cut of my staff cost', which violates the cost-paranoia psychology. Useful for a Y3+ enterprise tier (>10 staff) but never for the headline / Standard tier. Ignored.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Casual-sign-up threshold hardens at S$100/mo per Indie Hackers SG 2025-11 thread (n=14 commenters) + ASME-SBF SME Pulse 2025 (62% of <10-staff firms reject any new monthly recurring expense above S$100 without owner sign-off). DBS / OCBC SME survey series (2024) rank cash-flow as the #1 constraint for 45% of SG SMEs. At S$149+/mo the founder defers to next quarter or absorbs the DIY pain. NBA = S$160/mo means S$59 (Starter) is 0.37x — well inside the WTP gate's lower edge but pushes the buyer's intuitive 'cheap enough to try' signal. Walks at S$200/mo+.

Owner is already paying S$300-450/mo across 3-4 line items (Talenox + accountant + WICA broker + ad-hoc legal). Will pay if Elitez EOR consolidates the line items and reduces total spend by ≥30% — that's the threshold for 'worth switching'. NBA = S$365/mo, expected WTP S$149 = 0.41x (inside gate, deliberately below NBA to manufacture the 'less than half what I'm paying' decision-frame). Standard tier S$129 lands at 0.35x NBA; the Standard buyer treats this as a no-brainer once they see line-item consolidation. Walks above S$350/mo because at that point they'd consider hiring a part-time HR contractor.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: F&B / retail owner with WP/S-Pass mix
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Owner is paying ~S$850/mo across payroll SaaS + bookkeeper + WICA broker + S-Pass agent + their own time. Expected WTP S$349/mo = 0.41x NBA (cleanly inside the 0.4-1.2x gate); Pro tier list S$249/mo = 0.29x NBA, which the buyer reads as 'cheaper than my actual displaced spend' — the price-supporting anchor. Pro tier upsell (e.g. S-Pass new applications S$150/app) lifts effective ARPU into the S$300-400/mo zone. Walks above S$550/mo where the cost approaches a fractional in-house HR hire (~S$2-3k/mo at 0.1-0.15 FTE). High-stakes one-off events (missed WP renewal = S$5k fine + worker exits country) further support upper-stretch S$549.

Channel buyer: economics dominated by per-firm wholesale cost vs firm's own delivered cost. Existing all-in cost-per-firm is SGD 60-100/firm (incl. junior staffer time + SaaS seats); any wholesale price meaningfully below SGD 50/firm captures the channel. NBA = S$60/firm/mo; expected WTP S$45/firm = 0.75x NBA (clean inside the gate). Channel will negotiate aggressively — partner portal + bulk billing is more important than absolute price. Walks above SGD 75/firm because at that point the firm has no margin to mark up. Volume-tiered structure (50+ firms = S$35; 100+ firms = S$25) is expected and accepted.

recommended_tiers.name: Starter
what_in: Statutory payroll for 1-3 SG citizen / PR / WP employees
Monthly CPF EZPay submission as managed service (no founder login required)
Monthly itemised payslips (PDF + email push to employees)
Annual IR8A AIS submission to IRAS as managed service
Default WICA group cover via Elitez panel insurer (NTUC Income / Tokio Marine Asia / MSIG)
PDPA-compliant SG-resident hosting + audit log
30-min onboarding call with named Elitez account contact
what_excluded: Foreign worker (WP / S-Pass / EP) renewals + levy management (upgrade to Standard or Pro)
Casual / part-time payslip generation with hourly-rate logic (upgrade to Standard)
Annual employment-handbook + leave-policy refresh (upgrade to Standard)
Xero / QuickBooks / accounting-software bidirectional sync (upgrade to Standard)
WP / S-Pass concierge incl. MOM portal renewals + dependency-ratio tracking (upgrade to Pro)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Less than one Deel SG seat (~SGD 805/mo per employee) — for your entire firm, monthly statutory layer included. Less than your accountant's payroll add-on, with WICA bundled.

recommended_tiers.name: Standard
what_in: Everything in Starter, plus:
Statutory payroll for up to 8 SG citizen / PR / WP employees
Casual / part-time payslip generation with hourly-rate × hours-worked × age-band CPF logic
Xero / QuickBooks bidirectional sync (payroll journal auto-posts to GL)
Annual employment-handbook + leave-policy refresh (covers 2025 SPL expansion + Workplace Fairness Act updates)
Quarterly compliance review call with named Elitez account manager
Ad-hoc HR letter generation (employment confirmation, salary verification for visa/loan, contract templates) — first 4 letters/yr included
GST-registration trigger alerting + handover to Elitez Group corp-sec / accounting practice
what_excluded: WP / S-Pass / EP renewal + levy auto-debit + MOM portal management (upgrade to Pro)
Multi-jurisdiction (MY / ID / VN remote contractor) compliance (Year-2 SKU)
Concierge dispute / MOM-letter handling (upgrade to Pro)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Replace the Talenox + accountant + WICA broker + ad-hoc lawyer line items with one bill. ~70% less than an in-house HR hire. The line item your bookkeeper agrees with.

recommended_tiers.name: Pro
what_in: Everything in Standard, plus:
WP / S-Pass / EP concierge: levy auto-debit, MOM Self-Help Portal renewals, dependency-ratio tracking, COMPASS-points monitoring for EP
60-day-ahead pass-renewal SMS / WhatsApp push to owner with one-click submit
Statutory payroll for up to 15 SG + foreign-pass-holder employees including casuals / part-time
S-Pass / WP new-application support (4 applications/yr included; additional at S$150/app vs market S$300-500)
Workplace incident / injury claim assistance (WICA filing, MOM coordination, broker handover)
Mobile-first payroll dashboard (owner runs everything from phone after closing time)
Dedicated WhatsApp support line, business-hours SLA 1 hour
what_excluded: EDG / WSG WDG(JR+) workforce-redesign engagement scope (separate Elitez Group consulting fee, 50-70% rebate eligible)
Multi-outlet group restructure / payroll harmonisation (Concierge / custom)
Cross-border (MY / ID) staff hire under Elitez EOR MY / EOR ID (Year-2 multi-country SKU)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Less than one missed WP renewal fine (S$5k+). Less than the S-Pass agent retainer alone. Includes the WhatsApp line your operations actually run on.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤200 employees AND ≤S$100M annual revenue, vendor must be IMDA pre-approved for the relevant solution category. Hard pre-req: Elitez EOR is NOT yet on the IMDA HR/Payroll PSG catalogue (target submission 2026-Q3 to access 2026-Q4 SME budget cycle). Until listed, no PSG rebate is available; do NOT advertise PSG-net pricing in current marketing copy.

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, no headcount cap; project must be submitted via BGP under Human Capital Development pillar with measurable workforce-outcome deliverables. Applies to consulting wrap layered on the SaaS — not the subscription alone. Tier-1 'Singapore Together' priority sectors get 70% support.

grants.name: WDG(JR+) — Workforce Development Grant Job Redesign Plus
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding; engagement must be delivered by a WSG pre-approved JR+ consultant (Elitez Group is on the approved list, LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) and structured as a workforce-redesign project including training of in-house finance / HR team to maintain the platform. Applicable only to Pro-tier-and-above engagements where the consulting wrap is real.

grants.name: SFEC (SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit)
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥S$750 SDL contributions over qualifying period AND ≥3 SG local employees; stackable on top of EDG / WDG(JR+) for the training component of an engagement only, not the SaaS subscription itself.
